bot_token.description: Discord bot token (Bot User OAuth Token from the Discord Developer Portal). Stored plaintext in SQLite — masked in UI; do not commit to env or config.json.

allowed_dm_user_ids.description: Comma-separated Discord user IDs allowed to DM the bot. Empty = no DM access. IDs are 17-19 digit snowflakes from Discord's Developer Mode 'Copy ID'.

rate_limit_per_minute.description: Max user-initiated turns per minute per Discord user. Defends against runaway loops and noisy clients.

shared_telegram_chat_id.description: Identity mode. 0 (default) = Discord DMs get their own isolated conversation thread. Set to your Telegram chat id to SHARE one continuous conversation/history across both surfaces (DMs only; guild channels always stay isolated). Note: in shared mode, confirm-tier prompts render in Telegram, not as Discord buttons.

proactive_dm_user_id.description: Discord user id to DM proactive briefings/nudges/reminders to (mirrored from the same proactive jobs Telegram uses). Empty = the first id in allowed_dm_user_ids. The bot must share a guild with the user (or the user must allow DMs).

idle_disconnect_minutes.description: Disconnect the Discord gateway after this many minutes with no inbound messages (0 = stay connected). Useful on Pi targets.

taunt_mode.description: When ON, the voice channel plays the entrance and talking sounds below (a soundboard). When OFF, Modulus acts as a normal voice assistant instead: it listens for the wake word ("hey modulus" / "modulus") and you can talk to it like a normal chat. The two modes are mutually exclusive.

entrance_sounds.description: Taunt mode only. Comma-separated list of <user_id>:<absolute_path_to_mp3> for playing an entrance sound when a specific user joins a voice channel Modulus is in.

talking_sounds.description: Taunt mode only. Comma-separated list of <user_id>:<absolute_path_to_mp3> for playing a sound whenever a specific user starts talking in a voice channel.
